title: Glorious Tunisian Pork Belly Tagine
description: A winey and fluffy solo dinner dish featuring pork belly slices, this Tunisian tagine is a unique blend of flavors, and since it uses pork and wine, it is not satvik friendly, but can be adapted to be so by substituting these ingredients with satvik alternatives.
instructions: Preheat the sous vide machine to 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork belly slices with salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and cinnamon.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and sear the pork belly slices until browned on both sides.
Remove the pork belly from the skillet and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the red wine to the skillet and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Place the pork belly slices in a single layer in the sous vide bag and pour the wine sauce over the top.
Seal the sous vide bag and cook for 24 hours.
Remove the pork belly from the sous vide bag and slice into thin strips.
Serve the pork belly hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
